meaning: n.(可扩大射击范围的)棱堡,堡垒,阵地工事
examples: Whatever else it is, higher education is not a bastion of excellence.
I've even entered the last bastion of greendom—installing a composting toilet.
Like an iron bastion, the dam withstood the rushing floodwaters.
Founded in 1592, Trinity was for centuries a bastion of Protestant ascendancy in Ireland.
The injured two were evacuated by helicopter to the medical facility at Camp Bastion.
